title: Numbers at scale — NumPy
intro: NumPy does math on whole arrays at once — no loop needed — and it's what pandas, plotting and every science library is built on. Work through the notebook, then check in.
preview:
import numpy as np

readings = np.array([100, 112, 98, 120])
print(readings[readings > 100].mean())   # mean of just the high ones
question: The check-in builds 50 seeded readings, keeps those above 100, and prints their mean (2 decimals). What is it?
